data.title: Time is Getting Tight for Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) and Foundry Hopes
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) faces its sticking point. The stock has recovered marvelously from earlier losses, mostly on the strength of surging investment and foundry hopes. But those foundry hopes are about to be sorely tested, as Intel struggles to land customers for both the 18A and 14A processes. Investors are starting to get concerned as well, with shares sliding down nearly 5.5% in Monday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The "multiyear journey" that CEO Lip-Bu Tan declared is starting to look like a bridge too far to some investors, and concerns are starting to mount. The 14A production node is set to emerge, but so far, no major customers have really lined up to take advantage of it, reports noted. The 18A process is putting out chips—the Panther Lake lineup—but reports still suggest that buyers are a bit thin on the ground. Recent reports even suggested that Intel was going to put most of its production capacity to work on the Xeon chip line for servers.

Some deals have already been made, but no one is quite sure just what kind deals these are. Things like "…scope and timing…" are unclear at best. And the bulk of Intel's foundry revenue right now is, basically, Intel selling chips to itself. While Intel has landed substantial new investment and new hope to go with it, Intel needs to start making substantial sales wins, and do so at a time when its production yields are making its own products more scarce.

A Light in the Tunnel

There is one excellent potential deal in the making, assuming it actually comes to pass. Reports suggest that Apple (AAPL) is once again poised to come back to Intel to produce the M-series chip lineup. But this deal, if it comes to pass, would only be of limited help.

Reports suggest that the M-series chip would only start seeing production in numbers in 2027. That deal might expand to see iPhone chips made by 2028. But sales that are a year or two out do little good to a company looking for big sales wins right now.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on seven Buys, 19 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 122.13% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.04 per share implies 13.29% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed Ahead of Big Tech Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed Monday night after a strong start to a busy earnings week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.22% and 0.04%, respectively, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA)  futures were down 0.31% at 8:20 p.m. EDT on January 26.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Monday's session, stocks finished solidly higher, helped by gains in large technology companies. The S&P 500 climbed 0.5%, while the DJIA gained 0.6%. Meanwhile, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite rose 0.4% as Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT) moved higher ahead of their earnings reports later this week.

After the closing bell, shares of major health insurers sold off after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services proposed raising Medicare Advantage payments by just 0.09% in 2027. Humana (HUM) fell 12%, while CVS Health (CVS) slid nearly 10%.

At the same time, President Donald Trump said late Monday that he plans to raise tariffs on South Korean autos, pharmaceuticals, and lumber from 15% to 25%.

Investors are now focused on the Federal Reserve's policy decision this week. Rates are expected to stay unchanged, but markets will watch closely for signals on future rate cuts.

On the data front, traders will turn their attention Tuesday to the latest readings on consumer confidence and home prices.

Finally, on the earnings calendar, American Airlines (AAL) and Boeing (BA) are among the companies set to report quarterly results on Tuesday.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Housing Inflection Point Puts D.R. Horton (DHI) in Pole Position
data.description:
After a 2025 marked by high interest rates and deteriorating affordability in the U.S., the housing sector enters 2026 at a more subtle—but potentially constructive—inflection point. As the political debate in Washington brings housing costs to the center of the agenda and mortgage rates begin to ease at the margin, the focus shifts away from a full cyclical recovery toward when, and for whom, transaction volumes may return.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In this context, D.R. Horton (DHI) stands out as a particularly efficient way to express a bullish affordability trade in transition. This is not a broad, directional bet on housing; rather, it reflects the idea that even small improvements in the macro backdrop can translate into disproportionate operating leverage for the right builder. On that basis, I am adopting a Buy rating on DHI at current levels.

Affordability Is Becoming a Policy Variable

Housing affordability has become an extremely sensitive issue in the U.S., and increasingly one that is being treated as an explicit policy objective ahead of the upcoming midterm elections later this year.

Recent proposals by President Trump—including directing Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac (government-sponsored enterprises that support the private mortgage market) to increase purchases of mortgage-backed securities (MBS), as well as floating a temporary cap on credit-card interest rates—point to a coordinated effort to ease balance-sheet pressure on U.S. households. Ultimately, these measures aim to lower the effective cost of living for the average voter.

From a market perspective, this matters because housing affordability is now acting as a binding constraint. At current price levels, mortgage rates around ~6% sit very close to the threshold at which monthly payments become prohibitive for a large share of potential buyers. As a result, even a modest move toward ~5.5% could have a non-linear effect, improving payment-to-income ratios and reactivating household demand that has been sidelined by today's rate environment.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Raymond James Stays Bullish on Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Ahead of Q2 Earnings
data.description:

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Climb Despite Surging Shutdown Odds and Trump's Canada Tariff Threat
data.description:
In a show of strength, both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Monday, shrugging off rising odds of a government shutdown and President Trump's potential tariffs on Canada.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Senate Democrats have vowed to vote against a government funding bill that includes appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) after 37-year-old Alex Pretti was fatally shot by a federal agent in Minneapolis. A total of 12 bills are needed to fully fund the government, with six already approved. The remaining six have been grouped into one package, with Democrats proposing that funding for DHS be voted on separately in order to avoid a shutdown.

"Take up DHS by itself, let's have an honest negotiation, put some guardrails on what's going on, some accountability, and that would solve this problem," said Senator Angus King of Maine. The odds of a government shutdown have ripped higher to 81% from 10% on Friday on prediction platform Polymarket.

Minnesota Governor Tim Walz noted that Trump is considering reducing the number of federal agents in the state after the pair spoke over the phone on Monday morning. That comes after Trump announced he would send "Border Czar" and Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) veteran Tom Homan to assist with the crackdown in Minnesota.

On the tariff front, Trump threatened a 100% tariff on Canadian imports if the country signs a trade deal with China that involves lowering duties on the first 49,000 Chinese electric vehicle imports to 6.1% from 100%. He warned in a Truth Social post that China would "eat Canada alive, completely devour it."

It wasn't all bad news, as U.S. durable goods orders, which track demand for goods designed to last at least three years, signaled that companies may be looking past tariffs. New orders for November jumped 5.3% month-over-month, above the consensus estimate of 3.8%. The growth was driven by a strong showing in non-defense aircraft, with orders jumping by 97.6%. During the month, Boeing (BA) reported 164 orders, up from 15 in October. In addition, core durable goods orders, which exclude transportation, ticked higher by 0.5% and surpassed the estimate of 0.3%.

Looking ahead, the Fed will hold its first Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting of the year on January 28, where it is widely expected to hold rates steady amid signs of stabilization within the labor market.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.50% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.42%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Zoom Stock (ZM) Jumps 10% as Investment in Anthropic Estimated at $4 Billion
data.description:
Shares of Zoom Communications (ZM) are up 10% on Jan. 26 after analysts at Baird estimated the company's investment in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic could be worth as much as $4 billion.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In May 2023, Anthropic announced a partnership with Zoom and revealed that Zoom Ventures had invested in the company. The companies did not disclose the value of the investment at that time, but Zoom reported making $51 million in "strategic investments" that quarter.

Baird's analysts now estimate that investment went entirely to Anthropic. The startup is currently valued at $350 billion, suggesting that Zoom's stake could see a return of roughly 78 times its initial investment, according to the analysts at Baird. Those estimates put the current value of Zoom's investment in Anthropic at $2 billion to $4 billion, depending on dilution assumptions.

Anthropic's Rapid Growth

"While we (and the market) have been primarily focused on ZM's ability to gradually reaccelerate revenue growth and capture AI opportunities, the quieter, hidden gem might be its $51 million investment in Anthropic in 2023," wrote Baird in a note to clients.

Zoom, a video conference company, was popular during the Covid-19 pandemic. But its fortunes and share price have since faded. In contrast, privately held Anthropic has seen its growth and valuation explode over the past 18 months as the AI trade has accelerated. As such, Zoom's investment in Anthropic could prove to be a huge long-term benefit, writes Baird.

Is ZM Stock a Buy?

Zoom's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy, seven Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average ZM price target of $99.11 implies 4.78% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Analysts Like Nvidia's (NVDA) Latest $2B Investment in CoreWeave
data.description:
Wall Street has started to cheer chipmaker Nvidia's (NVDA) ai-factory-buildout">new $2 billion deal with AI cloud provider CoreWeave (CRWV). While Raymond James called it a "vote of confidence" in CoreWeave's AI cloud capacity, Needham sees future credit guarantees from Nvidia for CoreWeave's future leases for high-performance computing (HPC).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As part of the deal, CoreWeave is tasked with developing over 5 gigawatts of power capacity by 2030 by developing and operating AI factories using Nvidia's infrastructure, such as the Rubin platform, the Vera CPUs, and the Bluefield technology for storage systems. In return, Nvidia has agreed to buy Class A common shares in CoreWeave at $87.20 per share.

Needham Hammers Credit Guarantees for CoreWeave

The deal comes at a time when CoreWeave is battling pressure from investors despite topping Wall Street estimates in its Q3 2025 earnings results. Investors have dragged the Neocloud company to court over project delays in its AI infrastructure buildout, despite a recent update on the removal of the delay identified earlier.

Commenting, Needham emphasized how securing future leases from HPC operators had become difficult for CoreWeave due to concerns about its financial base and competition for hyperscaler deals, and how the deal could change that. CoreWeave builds and owns its data centers, but also leases additional sites from HPC operators.

"On the [sell-side analyst] call back [with CoreWeave], it became clear that NVIDIA would look to offer a backstop or credit guarantee on future leases to make CoreWeave more competitive with investment-grade hyperscalers," Needham clarified.

Davidson Sees Fewer Risks for CoreWeave in 2026

Chipping in, DA Davidson analyst Alex Platt noted that CoreWeave continues to enjoy strong demand for its computing capacity, with the deal helping to ease concerns about CoreWeave's vulnerability to third-party risks that have factored into its shares. In addition, Platt sees "multiple de-risking pathways" in 2026 for the New Jersey-based company.

He raised his CRWV price target by 62% from $68 per share to $110 and elevated the shares from Hold to Buy.

Raymond James Flags Earnings Potential for Nvidia

Meanwhile, Raymond James estimates Nvidia could earn between $60–$90 billion from selling systems and networking to CoreWeave between 2026 and 2030, representing about 40% of revenue from the deal, plus $9–$13 billion in graphics processing unit sales per gigawatt.

In addition, the financial services firm contended that Nvidia has a strong strategic reason to cultivate an ecosystem of independent customers and partners that rely on its chips to make its business less vulnerable to big cloud providers or hyperscalers that are building and using their own in-house AI accelerators.

Is CRWV Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, CoreWeave's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 13 Buys and 12 Holds issued over the past three months.

However, the average CRWV price target of $123.86 implies about 22% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
